column1: # Private Endpoint FQDN vs IP Access Experiment — Raw Results
# Certificate Issuer: Microsoft Azure RSA TLS Issuing CA 04
# All tests run from jumpbox VM (10.80.3.4) inside the VNet
# Runs: 5 per test case (all identical — deterministic config test)

column7: OK — cert validates against wildcard SAN
column11: Baseline: FQDN access via Private DNS Zone. DNS resolves to 10.80.0.23. All 5 runs succeed.
Direct IP — curl sends no SNI. Envoy resets TLS handshake immediately. curl exit code 35 (SSL connect error).
Direct IP with -k flag. Still fails — -k only skips cert verification but SNI is still missing. Issue is SNI not cert.
curl --resolve FQDN:443:IP makes curl send FQDN as SNI+Host but connect to 10.80.0.23. TLS and HTTP both succeed.
IP + Host header + -k. Still fails — -H Host: is HTTP-level (post-TLS). SNI still missing. Connection reset.
openssl s_client with FQDN. TLS handshake succeeds. Certificate chain returned.
openssl s_client to IP without -servername. Connection reset. No peer certificate.
openssl s_client to IP WITH -servername FQDN. TLS succeeds. Proves SNI is the sole gate.
HTTP port 80 direct IP without Host header. Envoy accepts TCP but returns 404 — no Host to route.
HTTP port 80 IP + Host header. Envoy matches app by Host header and returns 301 redirect to HTTPS.
